params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: days
params.description: The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: todate
params.description: Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: period
params.description:
Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Get all historical positions of a single vessel for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** speed

info.name: Vessel Historical Positions in an Area

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: days
params.description: The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: todate
params.description: Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: MINLAT
params.description:
Use with MAXLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MAXLAT
params.description:
Use with MINLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MINLON
params.description:
Use with MINLAT, MAXLAT, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MAXLON
params.description:
Use with MINLAT, MAXLAT, MINLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: period
params.description:
Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Get vessel historical positions for a single or for all vessels inside an area of your preference for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results
  • Information about

info.name: Vessel Positions of a Static Fleet

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 8 to get the latest

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned positions.  </br></br> Maximum value for terrestrial coverage is 60. Maximum value for satellite coverage is 180

params.name: shiptype
params.description:
Filter data by vessel type:
  • 2: Fishing
  • 4: High Speed Craf
  • 6: Passenger
  • 7: Cargo
  • 8: Tanker

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
  • full
If used with the value extended or full, the response includes scheduled static and voyage related vessel data report (AIS Message 5). In this case your request frequency might be limited (depending on your service terms)

If omitted, the returned records include only position reports (AIS Messages 1, 2, 3/ 18, 19)

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Keep a close eye on the vessel or group of vessels of your interest.

Notes
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** returned in (knots x10) and **TIMESTAMP** in UTC
  • **HEADING** for SAR Aircrafts contains the Altitude of the aircraft (“STATUS” = 97)
  • **HEADING** values -1 or 511 indicate lack of data
    • <

info.name: Vessel Positions in a Custom Area

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 1 to get the latest

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned positions. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: shiptype_generic
params.description: Filter vessels based on generic vessel types, comma separated values supported </br></br> Vessel type ID should be used and not vessel type name (2=Fishing / 4=High Speed Craft / 6=Passenger / 7=Cargo / 8=Tanker) </br></br> Should not be combined with shiptype_detailed filter

params.name: shiptype_detailed
params.description:
Filter vessels based on detailed vessel types, comma separated values supported

Vessel type ID should be used and not vessel type name - more

Should not be combined with shiptype_generic filter

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
  • full
If used with the value extended or full, the response includes scheduled static and voyage related vessel data report (AIS Message 5). In this case your request frequency might be limited (depending on your service terms)

If omitted, the returned records include only position reports (AIS Messages 1, 2, 3/ 18, 19)

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:

  • Information about AIS-transmitted data

  • **HEADING** for SAR Aircrafts contains the Altitude of the aircraft (“STATUS” = 97)
  • **HEADING** values -1 or 511 indicate lack of data
  • **SPEED

more
